input.prompt: My ASP.NET Core (.NET 8) app running on a Windows Server has slow requests under load. I have admin access. Which trace should I collect to capture waits/blocks, and what PerfView arguments should I use?

data.content: My ASP.NET Core (.NET 8) app running on a Windows Server has slow requests under load. I have admin access. Which trace should I collect to capture waits/blocks, and what PerfView arguments should I use?
data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-04-30T01:20:10.829Z</current_datetime>

My ASP.NET Core (.NET 8) app running on a Windows Server has slow requests under load. I have admin access. Which trace should I collect to capture waits/blocks, and what PerfView arguments should I use?

input.initialPrompt: My ASP.NET Core (.NET 8) app running on a Windows Server has slow requests under load. I have admin access. Which trace should I collect to capture waits/blocks, and what PerfView arguments should I use?

data.content:
Collect a **Thread Time (wall-clock) trace** — it captures both CPU and wait/blocked time (I/O, locks, async gaps).

**PerfView command:**

```
PerfView.exe collect /ThreadTime /NoGui /AcceptEULA /MaxCollectSec:30 /BufferSizeMB:512 /CircularMB:1024 /Providers:"Microsoft-DotNETCore-SampleProfiler" /Zip:true
```

**Key flags explained:**

| Flag | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `/ThreadTime` | Enables context-switch tracing (captures waits/blocks) |
| `/MaxCollectSec:30` | Limits collection to 30s — adjust to your repro window |
| `/BufferSizeMB:512` | Larger ETW buffer to avoid lost events under load |
| `/CircularMB:1024` | Ring buffer size for the trace file |
| `/Providers:"Microsoft-DotNETCore-SampleProfiler"` | Adds managed call-stack sampling |

**Analysis:** Open the `.etl.zip` in PerfView → **Thread Time Stacks** → filter to your process → look at `BLOCKED_TIME` and `Thread (Startup)` nodes to find contention or slow async paths.

> **Tip:** If you also want GC/allocation insight, append `/GCCollectOnly` or `/DotNetAlloc` to the flags. Avoid combining too many providers under heavy load — it can skew results.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about performance tracing for an ASP.NET Core .NET 8 app on Windows Server under load. They want to know which trace to collect and what PerfView arguments to use. This is a knowledge question — no tools needed beyond reporting intent.

Let me give a concise, actionable answer.
